src: Meghan's rebranded herself - and it's another valiant attempt to flog poshness to the little people

In these days of darkness we must take delight where we can find it, and one reliable source is the Duchess of Sussex. In her latest venture, Meghan, who occupies the tricky position of having a large platform and not enough to fill it (see also: Brooklyn Beckham), launched a new lifestyle brand that, she promised in a short video, would be "beautifully weaving together everything I cherish - food, gardening, entertaining, thoughtful living and finding joy in the everyday." The brand American Riviera Orchard is dead. Long live the new brand, As Ever.

The duchess and her husband, Prince Harry, have to do something and good luck to them, but it's hard not to study the rollout of As Ever for signs of the widening gap between Meghan's self-image and how the rest of the world sees her. ""As ever,"" writes the duchess on Instagram, "means "as it's always been" or some even say "in the same way as always,"" the "some" in this sentence apparently referring to the dictionary definition of a two-word phrase that no one has ever had trouble understanding.

It's ridiculous, of course, as is the hello-little-people tone adopted by the former royal highness, in keeping with the general attitude of the famous towards the unfamous - namely, one of stating the bleeding obvious as though sharing the key to existence. Still, I find myself sympathising with Meghan, who in her Instagram post and its accompanying video faces the stark challenge of having to pad out thin material. "This is what I do," she says to camera, "and I haven't been able to share it with you for the past few years, but now I can." What is the "it" here, and what has stopped her from sharing it? (It may involve jam, as did her previous brand, which she launched by sending "limited edition" jars of preserve to, among others, Kris Jenner, Mindy Kaling and Tracee Ellis Ross, who apparently also got a fresh bag of lemons.) Anyway, whatever the content, she hints darkly at some "whispers" abroad that forced her to jump the gun and share news of As Ever before she was ready - because "I wanted you to hear it from me first."

The fact there is no story here makes Meghan's insistence that she is getting in front of the story all the more enjoyable, but the more curious aspect of the announcement is the "you" to whom she refers and the idea she clearly has of her audience. The celebrity lifestyle space is crowded, ferocious and notoriously fickle, littered with the carcasses of former brand leaders such as Chrissy Teigen. Target and Bloomingdales distanced themselves from the star around the same time as she was accused of, and later apologised for, cyberbullying a woman called Courtney. The space is also dominated by market leaders such as Gwyneth Paltrow and Rachel Zoe.

Meghan is a minnow in these waters, but of course has her own secret weapon she isn't shy about using. In the new video, she deploys good old Harry to hold up the cameraphone and drop a few posh tones - "Is this thing on?" - to remind us that, even if As Ever isn't royal, exactly, it is at least royal adjacent. The new name is clearly a reach for something classier and more timeless than its predecessor, American Riviera Orchard, which sounded like a range of scented gift baskets you'd find at a discount shopping outlet, or the pair's charity arm, Archewell, a play on their son's name. Both brands have an aspirant lower middle-class vibe about them, which it would be rude to point out were it not for the fact that Harry and Meghan trade on class as part of their branding.

What they are selling isn't poshness itself, which strictly speaking should entail low-comfort, hole-in-the-elbows, bad cooking and draughty houses, but - much like Trump and his performance of "billionaire" - a kind of ersatz poshness they imagine might appeal to the peasants. It's a hard one to get right and, with the new brand name, Meghan seems to have changed course in the direction of, say, a Calvin Klein perfume or something a bit more abstract and upmarket. Although for my money she was on to more of a winner with the obscure and personalised brand name The Tig, her first attempt at lifestyle content that had to be shelved when she started dating Harry. As she explained at the time, The Tig was derived from an abbreviation of her favourite wine, tignanello, and had about it the confidence of something genuinely felt rather than a brand name that has been kicked through a comms team.

Which brings us to the duchess's understanding of audience. Discounting the forfeiture of sympathy that comes with money and privilege, there is something akin to pathos in the video that I find painful to watch. Meghan's aw-shucks, I-know-you-love-me tone assumes not only goodwill but abject fandom on the part of an audience which, I suspect, is at least 50% made up of those who regard her not only critically but with downright derision. It is hard to witness. "Sending lots of love," she signs off with noblesse oblige and an almost charming dedication to what she imagines we think of her.
tgt: Meghan on rebrändinud ennast - ja see on järjekordne vapper katse müüa lihtrahvale luksuslikkust

Nendel pimedatel päevadel peame rõõmustama seal, kus saame seda leida, ja üheks usaldusväärseks allikaks on Sussexi hertsoginna. Oma viimases ettevõtmises käivitas Meghan, kes on keerulises positsioonis, kus tal on suur platvorm, kuid mitte piisavalt sisu, mida selle täitmiseks kasutada (vt ka: Brooklyn Beckham), uue elustiilibrändi, mis lubas lühivideos, et see "kohendab ilusti kõike, mida ma kalliks pean - toit, aiandus, meelelahutus, läbimõeldud eluviis ja igapäevasest rõõmu leidmine." Bränd American Riviera Orchard on surnud. Elagu uus bränd, As Ever.

Hertsoginna ja tema abikaasa, prints Harry, peavad midagi tegema ja neile edu soovides, kuid on raske mitte analüüsida As Everi käivitamist, et näha laienevat lõhet Meghani enesepildi ja selle vahel, kuidas teda ülejäänud maailm näeb. ""As ever,"" kirjutab hertsoginna Instagramis, ""tähendab ""alati nii" või mõned ütlevad ""samamoodi nagu alati,"" ""mõned" selles lauses viitavad ilmselt kahe-sõnalise fraasi määratlusele, mida keegi pole kunagi mõistnud."

See on muidugi naeruväärne, nagu ka endise kuningliku kõrguse tere-väikerahvale toon, mis on kooskõlas kuulsate inimeste üldise suhtumisega mittekuulusesse - nimelt, ilmselge jagamine, justkui jagades eksistentsi võtit. Sellegipoolest leian end Meghani poole tundes, kes seisab oma Instagrami postituses ja selle juurde kuuluva videoga silmitsi karmi väljakutsega, et täita õhukest materjali. ""See on see, mida ma teen,"" ütleb ta kaamerale, ""ja ma pole suutnud seda teiega viimase paari aasta jooksul jagada, kuid nüüd saan."" Mis on see ""see"" siin ja mis on takistanud tal seda jagamast? (See võib sisaldada moosi, nagu ka tema eelmine bränd, mille ta käivitas, saates ""piiratud väljaande"" moosipurke teiste seas Kris Jennerile, Mindy Kalingule ja Tracee Ellis Rossile, kes said ilmselt ka värske sidrunipaki.) Igatahes, olenemata sisust, vihjas ta tumedalt mõnele ""sosinale"" välismaal, mis sundis teda As Everi uudiseid jagama enne, kui ta oli valmis, sest ""ma tahtsin, et te kuuleksite seda minult esimesena.""

Asjaolu, et siin pole lugu, muudab Meghani kindluse, et ta jõuab loo ette, seda veelgi nauditavamaks, kuid veelgi huvitavam on teada, et teate sisu on ""you"", kellele ta viitab, ja idee, mis tal oma publikust on. Elustiili brändide ruum on tihe, metsik ja kurikuulsalt kõikuv, täis endiste brändijuhtide, nagu Chrissy Teigeni, korjuseid. Target ja Bloomingdales distantseerusid staarist umbes samal ajal, kui teda süüdistati ja hiljem vabandati küberkiusamise eest naise nimega Courtney. Ruumi domineerivad ka turuliidrid nagu Gwyneth Paltrow ja Rachel Zoe.

Meghan on nendes vetes pisike, kuid loomulikult on tal oma salarelv, mida ta ei karda kasutada. Uues videos kasutab ta vana head Harryt, et hoida kaamerat ja öelda paar uhket sõna - ""Kas see asi on peal?"" - et meile meelde tuletada, et isegi kui As Ever pole täpselt kuninglik, on see vähemalt kuninglik lähedane. Uus nimi on ilmselgelt püüdlus millegi klassikalisema ja ajatumana kui tema eelkäija, American Riviera Orchard, mis kõlas nagu lõhnastatud kinkekorvide valik, mida võiks leida soodushinnaga ostukeskusest, või nende heategevuslik haru, Archewell, mis on nende poja nime mäng. Mõlemad kaubamärgid kannavad endas taotlevat keskklassi vibratsiooni, mida oleks ebaviisakas mainida, kui mitte asjaolu, et Harry ja Meghan kauplevad klassiga osana oma brändingust.

Mida nad müüvad, pole luksuslikkus ise, mis peaks rangelt öeldes hõlmama ebamugavust, aukudeid, halba toitu ja tuuletõmbusega maju, vaid - nagu Trump ja tema miljardärimäng - mingi asenduskulude luksus, mida nad arvavad, et talupojad võiksid meeldida. See on raske, ja uute kaubamärgiga tundub, et Meghan on muutnud suunda näiteks Calvin Kleini parfüümi või millegi pisut abstraktsema ja kallima suunas. Kuigi minu arvates oli ta võitnud rohkem oma isikupärase kaubamärgiga The Tig, oma esimese elustiili katse, mis tuli kõrvale jätta, kui ta Harryga kohtama hakkas. Nagu ta tol ajal selgitas, tulenes The Tig tema lemmikveini tignanello lühendist ja selle kohta oli midagi, mis tundus olevat midagi tõeliselt tunda, mitte kaubamärgi nimi, mis on läbinud kommunikatsioonimeeskonna.

See toob meid hertsoginna arusaamiseni publikust. Kui jätta kõrvale kahetsus, mis kaasneb raha ja privileegiga, on videost midagi, mis tundub olevat patos, mida on valus vaadata. Meghani ""aw-shucks"" ja ""I-know-you-love-me"" toon eeldab mitte ainult head tahet, vaid täielikku fännamist publikult, mis, ma kahtlustan, koosneb vähemalt 50% inimestest, kes vaatavad teda mitte ainult kriitiliselt, vaid lausa põlgusega. Seda on raske näha. ""Sending lots of love,"" lõpetab ta noblesse oblige'iga ja peaaegu võluva pühendumusega sellele, mida ta arvab, et me temast arvame.

src: Musk's Threats Darken as MAGA Rages at Fresh Legal Losses

If we get a court ruling saying that some of those firings are illegal, they'll say, We're not going to listen, we're going to keep firing. In a sense, they fired the watchdogs of waste, fraud, and abuse across the government, Trump did. The inspectors general, independent people in the agencies, Greg, [who] were there to watch Trump's wrongdoing. He had a Friday night massacre, wiped them out. He broke the law - you're supposed to give 30-day notice, you're supposed to give a reason.

That's a test. So far, there hasn't been litigation. Trump is waving the red flag. He wants to assert that absolute power. If there's a court order, that is the place where I expect Trump and Musk to say [something like] The Treasure of the Sierra Madre's "We don't care about no stinking badges," We don't care about no stinking court orders, this is my constitutional right. Article 3, that's the judiciary; Article 2, the President; Article 1, Congress. That's a place where Trump will say, Article 1, you got no business, Article 3, you got no business, this is my power. And out of all this mess, that's the single place where the Supreme Court might be most likely to take him seriously.

I think a lot of the other stuff is such a flagrant violation. These 40 cases.... It's like an advent calendar of illegality, Greg. Every day you turn up another flap and another constitutional provision statute or regulation is violated, started on day one. Many of these are dead losers for Trump, except with complicit, corrupt, conflicted Thomas and Alito.
tgt: Musk'i ähvardused erinevatel teemadel muutuvad järjest karmimaks, kui MAGA liikumine reageerib uuele ebaõnnestumisele kohtus

Kui me saame kohtuotsuse, mis ütleb, et mõned neist vallandamistest olid ebaseaduslikud, siis nad ütlevad lihtsalt: me ei kavatse kuulata, me jätkame vallandamisega. Oli mingis mõttes nii, et Trump vallandas kogu valitsuse ulatuses pettuste, raiskamise ja kuritarvitamise järel valva silma, tegi nii inspektorid üldiselt, iseseisvad isikud ametkondades, Greg, kes olid seisu võetud, et jälgida Trump'i vale tegusid. Ta tegi reedel õhtul massilise puhastuse ja kõrvalas nende kõik. Ta rikkus seaduse – selleks pidi andma 30 päeva ennetähtajalist teadet ja andma ka põhjuse.

See on kindel katsumus. Seni pole veel kohtuasju algatatud. Trump näitab punast lippu. Ta soovib tõestada oma absoluutset võimu. Kui kohtuorder siiski tekib, siis just seal ootan ma, et Trump ja Musk ütleksid midagi sarnast filmi "Sierra Madre mägede lõkk" tegelaste sõnadega: "Meile ei meeldi need tolmased märgid," "Meile ei meeldi need tolmased kohtuordrid, see on minu põhiseadusega tagatud õigus." Artikkel 3, see on kohtuvõim; artikkel 2, president; artikkel 1, kongress. Just seal punktis ütleb Trump, et artikli 1 all, teil pole siin midagi teha, artikli 3 all, teil pole siin midagi teha, see on minu võim. Ja kogu selle segaduse seast on just see see koht, kus Ülemkoht võib kõige tõenäolisemalt otsustada, et teda tuleb tõsiselt võtta.

Ma arvan, et suur osa teistest asjadest on lihtne ja ilmne seaduse rikkumine. Need 40 juhtumit... See on nagu adventikalender seadusvastasusest, Greg. Iga päev pöörad järgmise klapid lahti ja näed, kuidas rikutakse jälle üht põhiseaduse paragrahvi, seadust või määrust, alates esimesest päevast. Paljud neist juhtumitest on Trump'ile kindlad kaotajad, välja arvatud juhul, kui Thomas ja Alito on omalt poolt osalised, korrumpeerunud ja huvide konflikti all.

src: South African opener Matthew Breetzke etched his name into the record books by becoming the first batter to score 150 runs on ODI debut in second match of the ongoing tri-series against New Zealand at the Gaddafi Stadium on Monday. His stunning knock of 150 off 148 balls, laced with 11 fours and five sixes, surpassed the previous record held by the legendary West Indies' batter Desmond Haynes, who scored 148 in his maiden ODI innings against Australia back in 1978. Before Breetzke's innings, the highest score on ODI debut for South Africa was 124 by Colin Ingram against Zimbabwe in 2010. Now, Breetzke stands alone at the top, delivering a performance that showcased patience, aggression, and remarkable shot selection as South Africa posted a mammoth 304/6.

Fresh off a month-long stint in the SA20 2025 season, Breetzke seamlessly transitioned into the ODI format, forming a crucial second-wicket stand of 93 runs with Jason Smith (41). He reached his half-century off 68 balls and continued to build on his solid start, eventually reaching his maiden century in 128 deliveries with the help of six boundaries and two sixes. In doing so, he became only the fourth South African to score an ODI century on debut, joining an elite club featuring Ingram, his current captain Temba Bavuma, and Reeza Hendricks.

Post-century, Breetzke punished O'Rourke with two boundaries and a six in a single over, thus accelerating South Africa's late-innings scoring and building momentum for the Proteas. While his partnership with Smith provided early stability, he went on to forge another century stand for the fourth wicket with Wiaan Mulder (64), pushing South Africa past the 300-run mark. Breetzke, who will later join the Lucknow Super Giants in IPL 2025, exhibited complete dominance at the crease, enabling South Africa to establish a formidable total and providing their bowlers with a substantial platform in their bowling defence.
tgt: Lõuna-Aafrika avaja Matthew Breetzke lõi oma nime rekordite raamatusse, saades esimeseks mängijaks, kes lõi 150 lööki ODI debüüdil New Zealandiga toimuva kolmikvõistluse teise mängu ajal Gaddafi staadionil esmaspäeval. Tema hämmastav 150 lööki 148 palliga, mis sisaldas 11 nelikut ja viit kuuikut, ületas eelmise rekordi, mille püstitas legendaarne Lääne-India mängija Desmond Haynes, kes lõi 1978. aastal oma esimese ODI-mängu ajal Austraalia vastu 148 lööki. Enne Breetzke lööki oli Lõuna-Aafrika kõrgeim skoor ODI debüüdil 124, mille lõi Colin Ingram 2010. aastal Zimbabwe vastu. Nüüd seisab Breetzke üksi tipus, andes esituse, mis näitas kannatlikkust, agressiivsust ja märkimisväärset löökide valikut, kui Lõuna-Aafrika lõi tohutu 304/6.

Pärast kuu pikkust perioodi SA20 2025 hooajal sujus Breetzke'i üleminek ODI formaati sujuvalt. Ta moodustas koos Jason Smithiga (41) olulise teise wicket'i 93-punktilise seisu. Ta saavutas oma poolesajandi 68 palli ja jätkas oma kindla alguse arendamist, jõudes lõpuks oma esimese sajani 128 löögiga kuue piirjoone ja kahe kuuendikuga. Seeläbi sai temast vaid neljas Lõuna-Aafrika mängija, kes on debüüdi ajal ODI-sajandit löönud, olles osa eliitklubist, kuhu kuuluvad Ingram, tema praegune kapten Temba Bavuma ja Reeza Hendricks.

Pärast sajandit karistas Breetzke O'Rourket kahe piiriga ja kuuendikuga ühel üleminekul, kiirendades sellega Lõuna-Aafrika hilisemate löökide löömist ja luues Proteasele hoogu. Kuigi tema partnerlus Smithiga tagas varajase stabiilsuse, jätkas ta neljanda wicketiga Wiaan Mulderiga (64) veel ühe sajandi seisu, lükates Lõuna-Aafrika üle 300-punktilise piiri. Breetzke, kes liitub hiljem 2025. aasta IPL-is Lucknow Super Giantsiga, näitas täielikku domineerimist löögiväljakul, võimaldades Lõuna-Aafrikal luua muljetavaldava tulemuse ja pakkuda oma väravavahtidele märkimisväärset platvormi nende kaitses.

src: When Remaking a Masterpiece Is Worth the Risk

I've always thought that one of the most insoluble aesthetic problems going is remaking a movie masterpiece. I certainly understand the impulse to passionately re-engage such a work, but if the definition of a masterwork is something peerless at what it sought to accomplish, how do you remake it without simply reiterating it? There's a reason no one has tried second versions of Fellini's "8½" or Coppola's "The Godfather" or Polanski's "Chinatown." In the case of Fellini's achievement, is someone going to produce a more harrowing portrait of the self-deluding toxicity of male narcissism? In the case of Coppola's, of the corrosive effects of power? Or in the case of Polanski's, of the Hey-nothing-personal malevolence of late-model capitalism? (Water itself in that movie turns out to be the commodity that's manipulated for profit.)

When it comes to those who have waded into that kind of deep water, some have tried the Let's-really-shake-things-up solution. There's the lamentable 1962 remake of "The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari," for instance, which not only eliminated the central figure of Cesare the somnambulist but also featured a Caligari who instead of practicing diabolic hypnotism spent his time showing the heroine offensive pictures. Other filmmakers have chosen the even more baffling route of changing almost nothing, such as Gus Van Sant's nearly shot-for-shot 1998 remake of "Psycho." The number of disappointed moviegoers you risk in remaking a masterpiece from 1922 is smaller, for obvious reasons, but even so, the director Robert Eggers has made clear in any number of interviews his understanding that his new "Nosferatu" is re-engaging one of the greatest of the silent movies. (In 2016, when he was first attempting to remake the film, he told an interviewer that it felt "ugly and blasphemous and egomaniacal and disgusting" to take up that project so early into his career.)

F. W. Murnau's "Nosferatu: A Symphony of Horror" is itself a remake - an unauthorized adaptation of "Dracula," Bram Stoker's compulsively readable fever dream of a novel - and Murnau and the screenwriter Henrik Galeen retained much of what was arresting about the original while slipping in their own major changes: They're responsible, for example, for the now-set-in-stone tradition that sunlight can destroy the vampire, a notion nowhere in Stoker's book. I likely saw "Nosferatu" at too impressionable an age. I was 6, PBS was showing such things and my babysitter was simply glad I wasn't burning down the house. But I would have been flattened by it whenever I saw it. It was like having felt a draft from a grave. (Its effects were so long-lasting that 30-something years later I published a novel inspired by the film and its production.) The whole thing wasn't so much petrifying as insidiously unsettling, and all of that started with the figure of Nosferatu himself. Max Schreck's performance is, 102 years later, still the benchmark for sinister and dignified repulsiveness. Schreck's vampire has the stillness of a figure in a bad dream or a spider on its web, and the world he inhabits is at times equally disconcerting. After our hero Hutter's first frightening night in Nosferatu's castle, he notices in the mirror that something has bitten his neck, and he smiles.

But the most destabilizing figure might well be Hutter's wife, Ellen, our heroine, who's again and again shown to be telepathically on the monster's wavelength, even when he's thousands of miles away, so that polarities like good and evil or desire and repulsion seem to just evaporate while we watch. That last aspect alone would seem to land this story in Robert Eggers's wheelhouse. Part of the subversive energy of movies like "The Witch," "The Lighthouse" and "The Northman" derives from what feels like modern takes on historical characters in thrall to dark passions so that the distantly historical is both granted its strangeness and animated by a scrutinizing modern sensibility. At its best, his version both evokes and reconceives Murnau's most brilliant visual ideas. Murnau's masterful use of the opacities of dark archways from which the vampire can emerge and into which he can dissolve is both echoed and made new. Murnau's famously arresting use of shadows to visualize the vampire's defiling reach is reimagined when those shadows in this new version extend themselves in a 360-degree pan that evokes their vertiginous inescapability. And the shadow of the vampire's hand now extends across the entire city, repurposing the most memorable image from Murnau's "Faust."
tgt: Kui meistriteose ümbertöötamine on riski väärt

Ma olen alati arvanud, et üks kõige lahendamatumaid esteetilisi probleeme on filmi meistriteose uusversioon. Mõistan kindlasti impulssi sellise teose kirglikuks ümbertöötamiseks, kuid kui meistriteose definitsioon on midagi võrratut selles, mida ta püüdis saavutada, siis kuidas teha uusversioon ilma seda lihtsalt kordamata? On põhjus, miks keegi ei ole proovinud Fellini "8,5" või Coppola "Kummipapa" või Polanski "Chinatown" teistkordset versiooni. Kas Fellini saavutuse puhul suudab keegi luua veel ahistavama portree meeste nartsissismi enesepettusest ja mürgisusest? Coppola puhul võimu söövitavast mõjust? Või Polanski puhul hilisema kapitalismi hei-mitte-mitte-personaalse pahatahtlikkuse kohta? (Vesi ise osutub selles filmis kaubaks, millega manipuleeritakse kasumi saamiseks).

Kui tegemist on nendega, kes on sellisesse sügavasse vette astunud, on mõned proovinud Let's-really-shake-things-up lahendust. Näiteks "Dr. Caligari kabineti" kahetsusväärne 1962. aasta uusversioon, milles mitte ainult ei kõrvaldatud keskne tegelane Cesare somnambulist, vaid ka Caligari, kes diaboolse hüpnotiseerimise asemel veetis oma aega kangelannale solvavaid pilte näidates. Teised filmitegijad on valinud veelgi hämmastavama tee, kus nad ei ole peaaegu midagi muutnud, näiteks Gus Van Sandi 1998. aasta "Psühho" peaaegu pildistamisjärgne uusversioon. Pettunud kinokülastajate arv, kellega 1922. aasta meistriteose uusversiooniga riskite, on arusaadavatel põhjustel väiksem, kuid sellegipoolest on režissöör Robert Eggers teinud mitmes intervjuus selgeks oma arusaama, et tema uus "Nosferatu" võtab taas kasutusele ühe suurima tummfilmi. (2016. aastal, kui ta esimest korda üritas filmi uusversiooni teha, ütles ta ühele intervjueeritavale, et tundus "kole ja jumalakartlik ja egoistlik ja vastik" võtta see projekt nii varakult ette).

F. W. Murnau "Nosferatu: õudussümfoonia" on ise uusversioon - Bram Stokeri "Dracula", Bram Stokeri sundimatult loetava romaani palavikunägemuse omavoliline adaptsioon - ning Murnau ja stsenarist Henrik Galeen säilitasid palju sellest, mis oli originaalis köitev, lisades samal ajal oma olulised muudatused: Nad vastutavad näiteks selle eest, et nüüdseks on kivisse raiutud traditsioon, et päikesevalgus võib hävitada vampiiri, mida Stokeri raamatus ei ole kusagil. Tõenäoliselt nägin "Nosferatu" liiga mõjutatavas eas. Olin 6-aastane, PBS näitas selliseid asju ja mu lapsehoidja oli lihtsalt rõõmus, et ma ei põletanud maja maha. Aga ma oleksin sellest iga kord, kui ma seda nägin, plaksutanud. See oli nagu oleks tundnud hauast tõmmet. (Selle mõju oli nii pikaajaline, et 30 aastat hiljem avaldasin filmist ja selle tootmisest inspireeritud romaani). Kogu see asi ei olnud mitte niivõrd kivistav, kuivõrd salakavalalt häiriv, ja kõik see algas Nosferatu enda tegelaskujust. Max Schrecki esitus on 102 aastat hiljem ikka veel pahaendelise ja väärika eemaletõukavuse mõõdupuu. Schrecki vampiir on paigal nagu halva unenäo tegelane või ämblik oma võrgus, ja maailm, milles ta elab, on kohati sama häiriv. Pärast meie kangelase Hutteri esimest hirmuäratavat ööd Nosferatu lossis märkab ta peeglist, et midagi on tal kaela hammustanud, ja naeratab.

Kuid kõige destabiliseerivam tegelane on ehk Hutteri naine Ellen, meie kangelanna, keda ikka ja jälle näidatakse, et ta on telepaatiliselt koletise lainepikkusel, isegi kui ta on tuhandete kilomeetrite kaugusel, nii et sellised polaarsused nagu hea ja kuri või iha ja vastumeelsus näivad meie vaatamise ajal lihtsalt haihtuvat. Juba ainuüksi see viimane aspekt näib olevat Robert Eggersi pärusmaa. Osa selliste filmide nagu "Nõid", "Majakas" ja "Põhjamees" subversiivsest energiast tuleneb sellest, et ajaloolised tegelased, kes on tumedate kirgede küüsis, tunduvad olevat tänapäevased, nii et kaugelt ajalooline on nii võõrastatud kui ka elavdatud kaasaegse tundlikkusega. Parimal juhul meenutab tema versioon nii Murnau kõige geniaalsemaid visuaalseid ideid kui ka mõtestab neid uuesti. Murnau kasutab meisterlikult tumedate võlvide läbipaistmatust, millest vampiir võib välja tulla ja millesse ta võib lahustuda, ning teeb selle samal ajal uueks. Murnau kuulsalt hämmastav varjude kasutamine vampiiri rüvetava ulatuse visualiseerimiseks on taaslavastatud, kui need varjud laienevad selles uues versioonis 360-kraadises vaates, mis tekitab nende peadpööritavat väljapääsmatust. Ja vampiiri käe vari ulatub nüüd üle kogu linna, kasutades uuesti Murnau "Fausti" kõige meeldejäävamat pilti.

src: Sue Kay of Hawke's Bay, New Zealand writes: "I wish to thank all the kind people who helped my husband and me find our way from the airport to our hotel in the Sydney CBD. My husband is 90, and I'm 74 but, despite making ourselves known to our guide at the airport on arrival, we were left behind! We didn't know how to get to our hotel as we had the transfer organised. We must have asked at least six different people how to get to the Furama and everybody was so helpful and even helped us with our luggage. Sydneysiders may be big-city slickers but they have a small-town kindness!"

Some folks really do deserve a badge of honour for their pedantry (C8). Veronica Coyne of Springfield claims that "when bemoaning the loss of the express lane at Woolies "12 items or less," a friend told me she'd never used it on principle as it should have been "12 items or fewer.""

"Talk of badges reminds of the famous one from the 1960s-70s," says Merran Loewenthal of Birchgrove. ""It Begins When You Sink in His Arms and Ends with Your Arms in His Sink."" Jonty Grinter of Katoomba has one that reads "Chronically Advantaged" and adds that "it should be standard issue for all over seventy."

As we haven't heard from Tony Nicod of Collaroy for nine months, one could consider this offering as a radical re-entry: "Finally the kids are back at school. No more grommets in the water taking waves off us retirees who like to surf "gentlemen's hours" from 8am."

Readers will take a shine to this item from Janice Creenaune of Austinmer: "Thanks for the wonderful memories Hendrik Kolenberg (C8). I kept my father's beautifully constructed wooden valet box. It remains filled with old Kiwi polish and brushes (some admittedly a little dried). He brushed my shoes every day of my school life to a high shine as he always thought it a sign of character. (My husband and I did maintain the task with our own children.) And the valet box remains well stocked, albeit sometimes with those little sponge squeeze bottles as well."

Peter Miniutti of Ashbury identifies the utility value in buying the good stuff: "Before the advent of the Internet, I'm sure I once used the sturdy turnkey from a tin of Kiwi shoe polish to open a tin of spam."
tgt: Sue Kay Hawke’s Bayst, Uus-Meremaalt, kirjutab: „Soovin tänada kõiki lahkeid inimesi, kes aitasid minul ja mu abikaasal leida tee lennujaamast meie hotelli Sydney kesklinnas. Mu abikaasa on 90-aastane ja mina 74, kuid hoolimata sellest, et andsime lennujaamas oma giidile endast teada, jäeti meid maha! Me ei teadnud, kuidas hotelli saada, kuna transfeer oli meil organiseeritud. Küsisime ilmselt vähemalt kuue erineva inimese käest, kuidas Furama hotelli jõuda, ja kõik olid väga abivalmid ning aitasid meid isegi pagasiga. Sydneyslased võivad küll olla suurlinna inimesed, kuid neil on väikelinna lahkus!”

Mõned inimesed väärivad tõepoolest aumärki oma pedantsuse eest (C8). Veronica Coyne Springfieldist väidab: „Kui kurtsin Woolworthsi „12 toodet või vähem” kiirkassa kadumise üle, ütles sõbranna, et ta pole seda kunagi põhimõtteliselt kasutanud, sest see peaks olema „12 toodet või vähem” asemel „12 toodet või vähemate”.”

„Jutt aumärkidest meenutab kuulsat märki 1960.–70. aastatest,” ütleb Merran Loewenthal Birchgrovest. „„See algab siis, kui vajud tema embusesse, ja lõpeb siis, kui su käed on tema kraanikausis.”” Jonty Grinter Katoombast omab märki kirjaga „Krooniliselt eelistatud” ja lisab, et „see peaks olema standardvarustuses kõigil üle seitsmekümnestel”.

Kuna me pole Tony Nicodist Collaroyst üheksa kuu jooksul midagi kuulnud, võib seda pakkumist pidada radikaalseks tagasitulekuks: „Lõpuks on lapsed tagasi koolis. Enam pole väikseid surfaripoisse vees meie laineid ära võtmas, kui meie, pensionärid, tahame surfata „härrasmeeste aegadel” alates kella kaheksast hommikul.”

Lugejatele võib meeldida see lugu Janice Creenaune’ilt Austinmerist: „Aitäh imeliste mälestuste eest, Hendrik Kolenberg (C8). Hoian alles oma isa kaunilt valmistatud puidust kingakarpi. See on endiselt täis vana Kiwi kingavaha ja harju (mõned neist küll veidi kuivanud). Isa harjas mu kingi iga päev kogu mu kooliaja, sest pidas seda iseloomu märgiks. (Mina ja mu abikaasa jätkasime seda traditsiooni ka oma lastega.) Ja kingakarp on endiselt hästi varustatud, kuigi vahel on seal ka neid väikseid käsnaga pigistatavaid pudelikesi.”

Peter Miniutti Ashburyst toob välja kvaliteetsete asjade praktilise väärtuse: „Enne interneti tulekut kasutasin ma kindlasti kord tugevat Kiwi kingavaha konservikarbi avajat selleks, et avada üks spam-lihakarp.”

src: Meghan's rebranded herself - and it's another valiant attempt to flog poshness to the little people

In these days of darkness we must take delight where we can find it, and one reliable source is the Duchess of Sussex. In her latest venture, Meghan, who occupies the tricky position of having a large platform and not enough to fill it (see also: Brooklyn Beckham), launched a new lifestyle brand that, she promised in a short video, would be "beautifully weaving together everything I cherish - food, gardening, entertaining, thoughtful living and finding joy in the everyday." The brand American Riviera Orchard is dead. Long live the new brand, As Ever.

The duchess and her husband, Prince Harry, have to do something and good luck to them, but it's hard not to study the rollout of As Ever for signs of the widening gap between Meghan's self-image and how the rest of the world sees her. ""As ever,"" writes the duchess on Instagram, "means "as it's always been" or some even say "in the same way as always,"" the "some" in this sentence apparently referring to the dictionary definition of a two-word phrase that no one has ever had trouble understanding.

It's ridiculous, of course, as is the hello-little-people tone adopted by the former royal highness, in keeping with the general attitude of the famous towards the unfamous - namely, one of stating the bleeding obvious as though sharing the key to existence. Still, I find myself sympathising with Meghan, who in her Instagram post and its accompanying video faces the stark challenge of having to pad out thin material. "This is what I do," she says to camera, "and I haven't been able to share it with you for the past few years, but now I can." What is the "it" here, and what has stopped her from sharing it? (It may involve jam, as did her previous brand, which she launched by sending "limited edition" jars of preserve to, among others, Kris Jenner, Mindy Kaling and Tracee Ellis Ross, who apparently also got a fresh bag of lemons.) Anyway, whatever the content, she hints darkly at some "whispers" abroad that forced her to jump the gun and share news of As Ever before she was ready - because "I wanted you to hear it from me first."

The fact there is no story here makes Meghan's insistence that she is getting in front of the story all the more enjoyable, but the more curious aspect of the announcement is the "you" to whom she refers and the idea she clearly has of her audience. The celebrity lifestyle space is crowded, ferocious and notoriously fickle, littered with the carcasses of former brand leaders such as Chrissy Teigen. Target and Bloomingdales distanced themselves from the star around the same time as she was accused of, and later apologised for, cyberbullying a woman called Courtney. The space is also dominated by market leaders such as Gwyneth Paltrow and Rachel Zoe.

Meghan is a minnow in these waters, but of course has her own secret weapon she isn't shy about using. In the new video, she deploys good old Harry to hold up the cameraphone and drop a few posh tones - "Is this thing on?" - to remind us that, even if As Ever isn't royal, exactly, it is at least royal adjacent. The new name is clearly a reach for something classier and more timeless than its predecessor, American Riviera Orchard, which sounded like a range of scented gift baskets you'd find at a discount shopping outlet, or the pair's charity arm, Archewell, a play on their son's name. Both brands have an aspirant lower middle-class vibe about them, which it would be rude to point out were it not for the fact that Harry and Meghan trade on class as part of their branding.

What they are selling isn't poshness itself, which strictly speaking should entail low-comfort, hole-in-the-elbows, bad cooking and draughty houses, but - much like Trump and his performance of "billionaire" - a kind of ersatz poshness they imagine might appeal to the peasants. It's a hard one to get right and, with the new brand name, Meghan seems to have changed course in the direction of, say, a Calvin Klein perfume or something a bit more abstract and upmarket. Although for my money she was on to more of a winner with the obscure and personalised brand name The Tig, her first attempt at lifestyle content that had to be shelved when she started dating Harry. As she explained at the time, The Tig was derived from an abbreviation of her favourite wine, tignanello, and had about it the confidence of something genuinely felt rather than a brand name that has been kicked through a comms team.

Which brings us to the duchess's understanding of audience. Discounting the forfeiture of sympathy that comes with money and privilege, there is something akin to pathos in the video that I find painful to watch. Meghan's aw-shucks, I-know-you-love-me tone assumes not only goodwill but abject fandom on the part of an audience which, I suspect, is at least 50% made up of those who regard her not only critically but with downright derision. It is hard to witness. "Sending lots of love," she signs off with noblesse oblige and an almost charming dedication to what she imagines we think of her.
tgt: Meghan muutis oma brändi - ja see on järjekordne vapper katse piitsutada elegantsust väikestele inimestele.

Oma viimases ettevõtmises käivitas Meghan, kes on keerulises olukorras, kus tal on suur platvorm, kuid ei ole piisavalt, et seda täita (vt ka: Brooklyn Beckham), uue elustiili brändi, mis, nagu ta lühikese videoga lubas, oleks "kaunis kokku puitmine kõik, mida ma hoolin - toit, aiandus, meelelahutus, mõtlik elu ja rõõmu leidmine igapäevases". Bränd American Riviera Orchard on surnud. Elagu uus bränd As Ever.

Hertsoginna ja tema abikaasa, prints Harry, peavad midagi tegema ja palju õnne neile, kuid on raske mitte uurida As Ever'i väljalaskmist, et leida märke Meghan'i enesehinnangu ja ülejäänud maailma vaatevaate vahel laieneva lõhe kohta. "As ever", kirjutab hertsoginna Instagramis, " tähendab "nagu see on alati olnud" või mõned isegi ütlevad "sama moodi kui alati", "mõned" selles lauses ilmselt viitavad kahe sõnaga fraasi sõnaraamatuse määratlusele, mida kellelgi pole kunagi olnud raskusi mõistmisega.

See on muidugi naeruväärne, nagu ka endise kuningliku kõrguse poolt omaks võetud tere-väikeste inimeste toon, mis on kooskõlas kuulsate üldise suhtumisega kuuldamatute vastu - nimelt ühega, mis väidab, et verejooks on ilmselge, nagu jagades eksisteerimise võtit. Siiski leian end sümpaatilisena Meghan'iga, kes oma Instagram'i postituses ja sellega kaasas olevas videos seisab silmitsi õhukese materjali väljalülitamise väljakutsega. "See on see, mida ma teen", ütleb ta kaamerale, "ja ma ei ole suutnud seda teiega jagada viimastel aastatel, kuid nüüd ma saan. "Mis on siin" ja mis on takistanud teda seda jagamast? (See võib hõlmata džemi, nagu ka tema eelmine kaubamärk, mille ta käivitas, saades "piiratud väljaande" purke, et säilitada muu hulgas Kris Jenner, Mindy Kaling ja Tracee Ellis, kes ilmselt ka sai vihjeid sidrunite sisaldusest, mis iganes ta oli.)

See, et siin pole lugu, muudab Meghan'i nõudmise, et ta saab loo ees veelgi nauditavamaks, kuid kuulutuse huvitavam aspekt on "teie", kellele ta viitab ja idee, mis tal selgelt on oma publikust. Kuulsuste elustiili ruum on rahvarohke, julm ja kurikuulus, täis endiste kaubamärgi juhtide nagu Chrissy Teigeni laibad. Target ja Bloomingdales tõmbasid ennast tähe eest samal ajal, kui teda süüdistati ja hiljem vabandas Courtney nimega naise küberröövimise eest. Ruumi domineerivad ka turuliidrid nagu Gwyneth Paltrow ja Rachel Zoe.

Meghanil on oma salajane relv, mida ta ei häbene kasutada. Uues videos paneb ta hea vana Harry üles hoidma kaamerafonit ja laseb paari elegantset tooni välja - "Kas see asi on sisse lülitatud?" - et meile meelde tuletada, et isegi kui As Ever ei ole kuninglik, siis on see vähemalt kuninglik kõrval. Uus nimi on selgelt midagi klassikasemat ja ajatuumat kui selle eelkäija American Riviera Orchard, mis kõlas nagu mitmesugused lõhnavad kingituskorvid, mida leiad allahindlustusega kaubamajast, või paari heategevuslik käsivarre Archewell, mäng oma poja nimega. Mõlemal kaubamärgil on madalama keskklassi vibe nende kohta, mida oleks ebaviisakas näidata, kui see ei oleks asjaolu, et Harry ja Meghan kaubeldavad oma klassi brändimise osana.

See, mida nad müüvad, ei ole luksus iseenesest, mis täpsemalt öeldes peaks tähendama madalat mugavust, küünarnukidesse, halba toiduvalmistust ja õhukeseid majasid, vaid - sarnaselt Trumpile ja tema "biljonäri" esitlusele - mingisugune ersatz luksus, mida nad arvavad, et võiksid põllumeestele meeldida. Seda on raske õigesti teha ja uue brändi nimega tundub, et Meghan on kurssi muutnud näiteks Calvin Kleini parfüümi või midagi veidi abstraktsema ja kõrgemat.

See toob meid hertsoginna arusaamale publikust. Välja arvatud kaastunne, mis kaasneb raha ja privileegidega, on videol midagi, mis sarnaneb patosele, mida ma leian valusaks vaadata. Meghan'i aw-shucks, I-know-you-love-me toon eeldab mitte ainult head tahet, vaid ka alandlikku fändimist publiku poolt, mis, ma kahtlustan, koosneb vähemalt 50% neist, kes ei vaata teda mitte ainult kriitiliselt, vaid ka otsekohene pilk. See on raske tunnistajaks olla. "Saada palju armastust", kirjutab ta välja õilsusega ja peaaegu võluva pühendumisega sellele, mida ta arvab, mida me temast arvame.

src: When Remaking a Masterpiece Is Worth the Risk

I've always thought that one of the most insoluble aesthetic problems going is remaking a movie masterpiece. I certainly understand the impulse to passionately re-engage such a work, but if the definition of a masterwork is something peerless at what it sought to accomplish, how do you remake it without simply reiterating it? There's a reason no one has tried second versions of Fellini's "8½" or Coppola's "The Godfather" or Polanski's "Chinatown." In the case of Fellini's achievement, is someone going to produce a more harrowing portrait of the self-deluding toxicity of male narcissism? In the case of Coppola's, of the corrosive effects of power? Or in the case of Polanski's, of the Hey-nothing-personal malevolence of late-model capitalism? (Water itself in that movie turns out to be the commodity that's manipulated for profit.)

When it comes to those who have waded into that kind of deep water, some have tried the Let's-really-shake-things-up solution. There's the lamentable 1962 remake of "The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari," for instance, which not only eliminated the central figure of Cesare the somnambulist but also featured a Caligari who instead of practicing diabolic hypnotism spent his time showing the heroine offensive pictures. Other filmmakers have chosen the even more baffling route of changing almost nothing, such as Gus Van Sant's nearly shot-for-shot 1998 remake of "Psycho." The number of disappointed moviegoers you risk in remaking a masterpiece from 1922 is smaller, for obvious reasons, but even so, the director Robert Eggers has made clear in any number of interviews his understanding that his new "Nosferatu" is re-engaging one of the greatest of the silent movies. (In 2016, when he was first attempting to remake the film, he told an interviewer that it felt "ugly and blasphemous and egomaniacal and disgusting" to take up that project so early into his career.)

F. W. Murnau's "Nosferatu: A Symphony of Horror" is itself a remake - an unauthorized adaptation of "Dracula," Bram Stoker's compulsively readable fever dream of a novel - and Murnau and the screenwriter Henrik Galeen retained much of what was arresting about the original while slipping in their own major changes: They're responsible, for example, for the now-set-in-stone tradition that sunlight can destroy the vampire, a notion nowhere in Stoker's book. I likely saw "Nosferatu" at too impressionable an age. I was 6, PBS was showing such things and my babysitter was simply glad I wasn't burning down the house. But I would have been flattened by it whenever I saw it. It was like having felt a draft from a grave. (Its effects were so long-lasting that 30-something years later I published a novel inspired by the film and its production.) The whole thing wasn't so much petrifying as insidiously unsettling, and all of that started with the figure of Nosferatu himself. Max Schreck's performance is, 102 years later, still the benchmark for sinister and dignified repulsiveness. Schreck's vampire has the stillness of a figure in a bad dream or a spider on its web, and the world he inhabits is at times equally disconcerting. After our hero Hutter's first frightening night in Nosferatu's castle, he notices in the mirror that something has bitten his neck, and he smiles.

But the most destabilizing figure might well be Hutter's wife, Ellen, our heroine, who's again and again shown to be telepathically on the monster's wavelength, even when he's thousands of miles away, so that polarities like good and evil or desire and repulsion seem to just evaporate while we watch. That last aspect alone would seem to land this story in Robert Eggers's wheelhouse. Part of the subversive energy of movies like "The Witch," "The Lighthouse" and "The Northman" derives from what feels like modern takes on historical characters in thrall to dark passions so that the distantly historical is both granted its strangeness and animated by a scrutinizing modern sensibility. At its best, his version both evokes and reconceives Murnau's most brilliant visual ideas. Murnau's masterful use of the opacities of dark archways from which the vampire can emerge and into which he can dissolve is both echoed and made new. Murnau's famously arresting use of shadows to visualize the vampire's defiling reach is reimagined when those shadows in this new version extend themselves in a 360-degree pan that evokes their vertiginous inescapability. And the shadow of the vampire's hand now extends across the entire city, repurposing the most memorable image from Murnau's "Faust."
tgt: Kas klassikalise teose uusversioon on õigustatud?

Olen alati pidanud üheks kõige lahendamatumaks esteetiliseks probleemiks filmiklassika uusversioonimist. Ma mõistan kindlasti soovi sellise teosega kirglikult uuesti kohtuda, kuid kui klassikateose definitsioon on midagi, mis on ületamatu selles, mida see püüdis saavutada, kuidas seda siis uusversioonida, lihtsalt kordades? On põhjus, miks keegi pole üritanud teha Fellini "8½", Coppolale omase "Padrino" või Polanskile omase "Chinatown" teist versiooni. Fellini saavutuse puhul, kas keegi suudab luua veelgi südamepainavamalt enese petmise ja meheliku nartsissismi toksilisust kujutava portree? Coppolale omase puhul, korruptiivse mõju, mida avaldab võim? Või Polanskile omase puhul, hiljemal kapitalismil iseloomuliku, külma ja isiklikult mitteohaliku halastamatus? (Veest eneselegi selles filmis selgub, et kaubaks on see, mida kasumi saamiseks manipuleeritakse.)

Kui tegemist on nende inimestega, kes on sellega sarnaseid raskusi kogenud, on mõned otsinud radikaalset lahendust. Näiteks on olemas kahetsusväärne "Doktor Caligari kabineti" uusversioon aastast 1962, mis ei eemaldanud mitte ainult Cesare'i, unekäija, keskset figuurisid, vaid kujutas ka Caligarit, kes selle asemel, et tegelda saatanlikuks hüpnoosiks, veetis oma aega peategelasele solvavaid pilte näitamisega. Teised filmirežissöörid on valinud veelgi arusaamatuima tee – peaaegu midagi muutmata jätta, nagu Gus Van Santi "Psühho" uusversioon aastast 1998, mis oli peaaegu täpselt samasugune. 1922. aasta meistriteose uusversiooni tegemisega seotud pettumust tundvate kinokülastajate arv on ilmselgetel põhjustel väiksem, kuid režissöör Robert Eggers on mitmes intervjuus toonitanud oma arusaamist, et tema uus "Nosferatu" on üks suuremaid vaikfilmiklassikuid, mis saab uue elu. (Kuna ta esimest korda filmi uusversiooni kavandas 2016. aastal, ütles ta intervjuus, et selle projekti võtmine nii karjääri alguses tundus "vääratu, kohutava, egoistliku ja vastikuna".)

F.W. Murnau film "Nosferatu: Hõrje Sümfoonia" on ise versioon – volitamata kohandamine Bram Stokeri lugemisväärsest romaanist "Dracula" – ja Murnau ning stssenarist Henrik Galeen säilitasid palju algupärase võlu, kuid tegid samas ka suuri muutusi. Näiteks on just nemad need, kes on loonud nüüdseks kindlaks kujunenud traditsiooni, et päikesevalgus võib vampiiri hävitada, mis Stokeri raamatus ei mainita. Ilmselt nägin ma "Nosferatut" liiga noorelt. Olin kuus aastat vana, PBS näitas selliseid filme ja minu lastehoidja oli lihtsalt õnnelik, et ma ei süüdanud maja põlema. Kuid ma oleksin igas vanuses selle mõju all. See oli nagu oleksin tundnud tuulepuhangut hauale. (Selle mõju oli nii püsiv, et 30 aasta pärast avaldasin filmist ja selle tootmisest inspireeritud romaani.) Kogu film ei olnud niivõrd hirmutav kui pigem salakavalalt segastav, ja kõik algas Nosferatu enda figuurist. Max Schrecki esitus on 102 aasta möödumise järel endiselt eeskuju süngetele ja väärikatele vastikusele. Schrecki vampiiril on unenäo või ämbliku oma võrgus oleva figuuri seisak, ja tema maailm on kohati sama ebaselge. Pärast meie kangelase Hutteri esimest kohutavat ööd Nosferatu lossis märkab ta peeglis, et midagi on tema kaela hammustanud, ja ta naeratab.

Kuid kõige destabiliseerivaks kujuks võib-olla on Hutteri naine, Ellen, meie kangelitar, keda on korduvalt näidatud telepaatiliselt koos monstriga, isegi kui ta on tuhandete kilomeetrite kaugusel, nii et sellised polaarsed vastandmõisted nagu hea ja kuri või soov ja vastumeel lihtsalt aurustuvad meie silme ees. See viimane aspekt üksi paistab selle loo asetavat Robert Eggersi loomingu valda. Osal sellest subversiivsest energiast filmides nagu "Nõia", "Tuletorn" ja "Põhjamees" pihtib see, et nad kujutavad ajaloolisi tegelasi, kes on pimedate passionide võluses, nii et kaugenev ajalooline on nii omadega võõras kui ka kaasaegse kriitilise pilguga elavdatud. Parimal võimalikul viisil nii taastab kui ka mõtestab ta uuesti Murnau säravaimad visuaalsed ideed. Murnau meisterlik kasutamine tumedatele arkhividele, millest vampiir võib ilmuda ja kuhu ta võib kaokseda, on nii kajastatud kui ka uuendatud. Murnau kuulus, silmapaistev varjude kasutamine vampiiri korrumpeeriva ulatuse kujutamiseks on kujutatud nii, et need varjud ulatuvad 360-kraadise panoraamvaatega, tekitades peapööritust. Ja vampiiri käe varj on nüüd laienenud üle kogu linna, kasutades taas ära meeldetuletavaima kujutise Murnau "Faustist".

src: Musk's Threats Suddenly Darken as Trump Legal Losses Trigger MAGA Fury

Rage is mounting among President Donald Trump's allies over the losses that Trump's agenda has suffered in court, which are clearly becoming a serious obstacle to him. It's gotten so bad that Elon Musk shared a tweet from someone who suggested that it may be time to defy the courts. Musk also boosted Trump allies who are seething over these rulings.
